hazard.summary: Intermittent HTTP 429 during Azure PrivateEndpoint creation; reducing Pulumi parallelism (--parallel 1) mitigates, suggesting burst/concurrency exceeds an API/provider budget even after retries.
signals: Status=429 Code="RetryableError" Message="A retryable error occurred."
We automatically retry responses with status code 429 so this error is occurring after we retried with a back-off strategy
expose the retry parameters via provider configuration / environment variables

constraints: Concurrency for PrivateEndpoint create/update must be capped (globally or per-resource) to stay within throttling limits under burst provisioning.
429 handling must be bounded by a retry budget (max attempts and/or max elapsed time) to avoid unbounded churn.
Backoff must honor server hints when present (Retry-After) and include jitter to reduce synchronized retry storms.
When a 429 surfaces after retries, debug logs must emit a compact retry summary (attempts, total elapsed retry time, and last Retry-After seen if any).
verification: Repro: create PrivateEndpoint with parallelism N>1 and record frequency of 429.
Mitigation: set --parallel 1 (or provider-level concurrency cap) and confirm 429 disappears or drops sharply.
If retry knobs are exposed: increase retry budget/backoff and confirm reduced 429 surfacing under controlled burst.
Instrumentation: confirm debug output includes retry summary when 429 occurs (attempts, elapsed, last Retry-After if present).
